message:
feat(brenner-loop): add Session Dashboard Layout (reew.1)

SessionDashboard component with:
- PhaseTimeline: Visual progress through 11 session phases
- PhaseContent: Container for phase-specific content
- BrennerQuote: Contextual Sydney Brenner quotes per phase
- HypothesisCard sidebar integration
- Navigation footer with Previous/Next buttons
- Responsive grid layout (2/3 + 1/3 on desktop, stacked on mobile)
- Framer Motion animations for phase transitions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -386,7 +386,7 @@
 {"id":"brenner_bot-an1n.3","title":"Create HypothesisCard React Component","description":"# Create HypothesisCard React Component

## Purpose
Build the primary UI component for displaying and interacting with hypothesis cards.

## Design
The card should show:
1. **Statement** - The hypothesis text (prominent)
2. **Mechanism** - Proposed causal pathway
3. **Confidence** - Score with visual indicator
4. **Version** - Version number with history link
5. **Discriminative Structure** - Collapsible section showing:
   - Predictions if true
   - Predictions if false
   - Falsification conditions
6. **Confounds** - Identified weaknesses
7. **Actions** - Edit, evolve, view history

## Visual Design
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  H₂ (v3)                              Confidence: 47%│
│  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  [████░░░░░░] │
│                                                      │
│  Algorithm-driven content selection causes increased │
│  clinical depression in teenagers                    │
│                                                      │
│  Mechanism: Recommendation algorithm → exposure to   │
│  comparison content → negative self-evaluation → ... │
│                                                      │
│  ▼ Discriminative Structure                          │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │ ✓ If true: Depression ↑ with algorithm changes   ││
│  │ ✗ If false: No temporal correlation             ││
│  │ ⊘ Would falsify: Pre-change depression rise     ││
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│                                                      │
│  ⚠ 2 confounds identified                           │
│                                                      │
│  [Edit] [View History] [Evolve]                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Component API
```typescript
interface HypothesisCardProps {
  hypothesis: HypothesisCard;
  mode: 'view' | 'edit' | 'compact';
  onEdit?: (updates: Partial<HypothesisCard>) => void;
  onEvolve?: () => void;
  onViewHistory?: () => void;
  showConfounds?: boolean;
  showStructure?: boolean;
}
```

## File Location
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/HypothesisCard.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Renders all hypothesis fields
- [ ] Confidence visualized (progress bar or similar)
- [ ] Discriminative structure expandable
- [ ] Edit mode allows modification
- [ ] Responsive design works on mobile

Build succeeds.","labels":["brenner-loop","hypothesis-engine","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-an1n.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-an1n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:27:06.684760858-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-an1n.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-an1n.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:34:53.596636838-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-an1n.4","title":"Implement Robustness Score Calculation","description":"# Implement Robustness Score Calculation

## Purpose
Quantify how well a hypothesis has survived testing, providing a meaningful confidence indicator.

## Why This Matters
Confidence shouldn't be vibes. A hypothesis that survives strong discriminative tests is genuinely more robust than one that's never been challenged.

## Robustness Score Structure
```typescript
interface RobustnessScore {
  overall: number;  // 0-100
  
  components: {
    testsAttempted: number;
    testsSurvived: number;
    testsFailed: number;
    
    // Weighted by discriminative power
    weightedSurvival: number;
    
    // Structural quality
    falsifiabilityScore: number;  // How testable is it? (0-100)
    specificityScore: number;     // How precise is the prediction? (0-100)
  };
  
  interpretation: 'robust' | 'shaky' | 'falsified' | 'untested';
}
```

## Calculation Logic
```typescript
function computeRobustness(
  hypothesis: HypothesisCard,
  evidenceLedger: EvidenceEntry[]
): RobustnessScore {
  // Get all tests for this hypothesis version
  const tests = evidenceLedger.filter(e => 
    e.hypothesisVersion === hypothesis.id
  );
  
  // Weight by discriminative power
  const survivalScore = tests
    .filter(t => t.result === 'supports')
    .reduce((sum, t) => sum + t.test.discriminativePower, 0);
  
  const totalPower = tests
    .reduce((sum, t) => sum + t.test.discriminativePower, 0);
  
  // Structural quality
  const falsifiabilityScore = 
    hypothesis.impossibleIfTrue.length > 0 ? 80 : 20;
  
  const specificityScore = 
    computeSpecificity(hypothesis.predictionsIfTrue);
  
  // Handle untested case
  if (totalPower === 0) {
    return {
      overall: 50,
      interpretation: 'untested',
      // ...
    };
  }
  
  const weightedSurvival = survivalScore / totalPower;
  
  return {
    overall: weightedSurvival * 60 + falsifiabilityScore * 0.2 + specificityScore * 0.2,
    interpretation: 
      tests.some(t => t.result === 'falsifies') ? 'falsified' :
      weightedSurvival > 0.7 ? 'robust' : 'shaky',
    // ...
  };
}
```

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/robustness.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Score calculated correctly from evidence
- [ ] Handles untested hypotheses gracefully
- [ ] Interpretation labels make sense
- [ ] UI can display score meaningfully

 {"id":"brenner_bot-an1n.5","title":"Build Hypothesis Intake Form (Socratic Refinement)","description":"# Build Hypothesis Intake Form (Socratic Refinement)

## Purpose
Create the initial hypothesis entry experience that guides users through proper formulation.

## The Problem
Most hypotheses arrive malformed:
- "Social media causes depression"
- "AI will take all jobs"
- "Meditation is good for you"

These are vague, non-falsifiable, and confound multiple mechanisms.

## The Socratic Approach
Guide users through refinement:

### Step 1: Initial Statement
> "Enter your hypothesis"
> [                                    ]

### Step 2: Mechanism
> "What specific mechanism are you proposing?"
> □ Content exposure → mood change
> □ Social comparison → self-esteem
> □ Displacement of other activities
> □ Algorithm-driven engagement loops
> □ Other: [________________]

### Step 3: Predictions If True
> "If your hypothesis is CORRECT, what would we observe?"
> [Add observations that MUST be true if you're right]

### Step 4: Predictions If False
> "If your hypothesis is WRONG, what would we observe?"
> [Add observations that would indicate you're wrong]

### Step 5: Falsification Conditions (Critical)
> "What observation would PROVE YOU WRONG — not just weaken
>  your hypothesis, but definitively rule it out?"
> 
> This is crucial. If you can't answer, your hypothesis
> isn't testable yet.
> 
> [Add at least one falsification condition]

### Step 6: Initial Confidence
> "How confident are you? (0-100)"
> [Slider or input]
> 
> Note: High confidence should mean you've already
> thought about how you could be wrong.

## UI Pattern
Multi-step wizard with:
- Progress indicator
- Previous/Next navigation
- Brenner quotes relevant to each step
- Validation before proceeding

## File Location
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/HypothesisIntake.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Multi-step wizard works smoothly
- [ ] Validation enforces required fields
- [ ] Can't skip falsification step
- [ ] Brenner quotes shown at relevant moments
- [ ] Mobile-friendly

-{"id":"brenner_bot-an1n.6","title":"FEATURE: Competing Hypotheses Arena - Head-to-Head Comparison","description":"# Competing Hypotheses Arena - Head-to-Head Comparison

## The Gap This Fills
The current design tracks a single hypothesis through evolution. But Brenner's approach fundamentally involves **comparing competing hypotheses against each other**:

> "The question is not whether your hypothesis is consistent with the data, but whether it's MORE consistent than the alternatives." — paraphrased from §89

Without competing hypotheses, users default to confirmation bias: "Does my hypothesis fit?" instead of "Does my hypothesis fit BETTER than alternatives?"

## Feature Design

### Hypothesis Arena
```typescript
interface HypothesisArena {
  id: string;
  question: string;  // The research question being addressed
  
  // Competing hypotheses
  competitors: HypothesisCard[];
  
  // Shared evidence
  sharedEvidence: EvidenceEntry[];  // Tests that apply to all
  
  // Comparison matrix
  testResults: {
    testId: string;
    hypothesisId: string;
    result: 'supports' | 'challenges' | 'neutral';
  }[];
  
  // Rankings
  currentRanking: { hypothesisId: string; score: number }[];
  eliminatedHypotheses: { hypothesisId: string; eliminatedBy: string }[];
}
```

### Key Features

#### 1. Multiple Hypothesis Entry
When Object Transpose generates alternatives, they become REAL competitors:
- H₀: Original hypothesis
- H₁: Reverse causation version  
- H₂: Third variable version
- H₃: User-added alternative

Each gets tracked as a first-class hypothesis with its own predictions.

#### 2. Shared Test Application
When recording evidence, user selects which hypotheses the test applies to:
- "This test discriminates between H₀ and H₁"
- "This test challenges both H₀ and H₂"
- "This test supports H₁ but is neutral to others"

#### 3. Elimination Tracking
When a hypothesis fails a strong test:
- Mark as "Eliminated"
- Record which test eliminated it
- Show in the "Graveyard" (see related feature)
- Celebrate the narrowing of possibility space

#### 4. Comparison Matrix View
```
            Test 1    Test 2    Test 3    Status
H₀          ✓ +5%     ✗ -20%    ?         Active (52%)
H₁          ✓ +8%     ✓ +5%     ?         Active (71%)
H₂          ✗ -15%    —         —         Eliminated
```

#### 5. Brenner Scoring
Hypotheses that make BOLD predictions should get bonus credit:
- Hypothesis predicted something specific → higher multiplier
- Hypothesis hedged → lower multiplier
- "The bolder the prediction, the more informative the test"

### Integration Points
- Object Transpose → Creates initial competitors
- Evidence Ledger → Records per-hypothesis results
- Agent Tribunal → Evaluates all competitors
- Research Brief → Shows elimination history

## Why This is Critical
Without competing hypotheses, the system enables confirmation bias. With them, it forces discriminative thinking — the core Brenner insight.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Can have 2+ hypotheses competing on same question
- [ ] Tests can be applied across hypotheses
- [ ] Elimination is tracked and visible
- [ ] Comparison matrix shows relative standings
- [ ] Eliminated hypotheses are preserved for learning

 {"id":"brenner_bot-an1n.7","title":"FEATURE: Hypothesis Graveyard - Learning from Falsification","description":"# Hypothesis Graveyard - Learning from Falsification

## The Gap This Fills
When a hypothesis is falsified, the current system just updates confidence to near-zero. But Brenner would say:

> "Being proven wrong is not a failure — it's the most informative outcome. You've eliminated a possibility and narrowed the search space."

The system should CELEBRATE falsification and extract maximum learning value.

## Feature Design

### Graveyard Data Model
```typescript
interface FalsifiedHypothesis {
  id: string;
  hypothesis: HypothesisCard;
  
  // Death certificate
  falsifiedAt: Date;
  killingBlow: EvidenceEntry;  // The test that did it
  deathType: DeathType;
  
  // Learning extraction
  lessonsLearned: string[];
  whatWeNowKnow: string[];
  whatRemainsOpen: string[];
  
  // Legacy
  successorHypotheses: string[];  // What came next
  contributedTo: string[];        // Other hypotheses informed by this death
  
  // Epitaph
  epitaph: string;  // User's summary of what was learned
  brennerQuote?: string;  // Relevant Brenner wisdom
}

type DeathType = 
  | 'direct_falsification'    // Impossible observation occurred
  | 'mechanism_failure'       // Proposed pathway doesn't work
  | 'effect_size_collapse'    // Effect is too small to matter
  | 'superseded'              // Better hypothesis emerged
  | 'unmeasurable'            // Can't be tested with current methods
  | 'scope_reduction';        // Valid only in narrow conditions
```

### Key Features

#### 1. Dignified Death Ceremony
When a hypothesis is falsified:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  🪦 HYPOTHESIS FALSIFIED                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  "Algorithm-driven content selection causes clinical        │
│   depression in teenagers"                                  │
│                                                             │
│  Killed by: Natural Experiment - Algorithm Change 2021     │
│  Observation: Depression rates rose BEFORE algorithm change│
│                                                             │
│  Brenner says: "This is not a failure. This is progress.  │
│  You now know something you didn't before."                │
│                                                             │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│                                                             │
│  What did we learn?                                        │
│  [                                                    ]    │
│                                                             │
│  What remains open?                                        │
│  [                                                    ]    │
│                                                             │
│  What should we try next?                                  │
│  ○ Reverse causation hypothesis                            │
│  ○ Third variable hypothesis                               │
│  ○ Refined mechanism hypothesis                            │
│  ○ Something else: [                              ]        │
│                                                             │
│  [Generate Successor Hypothesis] [Archive to Graveyard]    │
│                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### 2. Graveyard Gallery
View all falsified hypotheses:
- Organized by topic/domain
- Searchable
- Shows what was learned from each
- Links to successor hypotheses

#### 3. Learning Extraction
Prompt users to document:
- What assumption was wrong?
- What does this tell us about the phenomenon?
- What's still possible?
- What new questions arise?

#### 4. Failure Pattern Analysis
Across sessions:
- "50% of psychology hypotheses fail due to reverse causation"
- "Most failures happen at the mechanism test"
- "Users who do Level Split first have fewer failures"

#### 5. Graveyard Achievements
Gamification that celebrates learning:
- 🪦 "First Blood" - First hypothesis falsified
- ⚰️ "Hypothesis Hunter" - 5 hypotheses properly falsified
- 🎓 "Learned Humility" - Revised hypothesis after falsification
- 🔬 "Scientific Method" - Falsification led to better hypothesis

### Why This is Critical
Most tools hide failure. This tool should celebrate it because:
- Falsification is the most informative outcome
- Learning from failure prevents repeat mistakes
- Graveyard creates institutional memory
- Celebrating failure reduces ego investment

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Falsified hypotheses get dignified ceremony
- [ ] Learning extraction is prompted
- [ ] Graveyard is searchable and browsable
- [ ] Successor hypotheses are linked
- [ ] Failure patterns are analyzed

 {"id":"brenner_bot-an1n.8","title":"FEATURE: Assumption Ladder - Dependency Mapping & Cascade Analysis","description":"# Assumption Ladder - Dependency Mapping & Cascade Analysis

## The Gap This Fills
The HypothesisCard has an "assumptions" field, but it's just a list. Missing:
- Which assumptions are load-bearing?
- If assumption A fails, does the whole hypothesis fail?
- Can assumptions be tested independently?
- What's the cascade of failure?

## Feature Design

### Assumption Structure
```typescript
interface Assumption {
  id: string;
  hypothesisId: string;
  
  // Content
  statement: string;
  rationale: string;  // Why do we assume this?
  
  // Classification
  type: AssumptionType;
  criticality: 'foundational' | 'important' | 'minor';
  
  // Dependencies
  dependsOn: string[];  // Other assumption IDs
  failureCascade: string[];  // What fails if this fails
  
  // Testability
  testable: boolean;
  testStrategy?: string;
  linkedTests?: string[];  // Test IDs
  
  // Status
  status: 'assumed' | 'tested' | 'validated' | 'invalidated';
  confidence: number;
  
  // Evidence
  supportingEvidence?: string[];
  challengingEvidence?: string[];
}

type AssumptionType = 
  | 'causal_structure'   // X causes Y (not reverse)
  | 'measurement'        // We can measure X accurately
  | 'population'         // Effect applies to this population
  | 'mechanism'          // This is how X causes Y
  | 'temporal'           // Timing assumptions
  | 'scope'              // Effect applies in these conditions
  | 'background'         // Implicit assumptions
  | 'methodological';    // Assumptions about our approach
```

### Assumption Ladder View
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ASSUMPTION LADDER                                          │
│  Your hypothesis rests on these assumptions                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  ⚠️ FOUNDATIONAL (if wrong, hypothesis fails)               │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ A1: Causation runs X → Y (not Y → X)                  │ │
│  │ Status: Assumed (untested)                            │ │
│  │ If wrong: Entire hypothesis invalid                   │ │
│  │ [Test This] [Mark Validated]                          │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ A2: Depression can be validly measured by PHQ-9      │ │
│  │ Status: Validated (established in literature)         │ │
│  │ If wrong: Outcome measurement invalid                 │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                             │
│  ⚡ IMPORTANT (if wrong, hypothesis weakened)               │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ A3: Effect applies to all teens (not just subgroup)  │ │
│  │ Status: Assumed (untested)                            │ │
│  │ If wrong: Hypothesis scope reduced                    │ │
│  │ [Test This]                                           │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                             │
│  📝 MINOR (if wrong, minor adjustment needed)               │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ A4: Effect is linear (not threshold-based)           │ │
│  │ Status: Assumed                                       │ │
│  │ [Test This]                                           │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                             │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│  DEPENDENCY MAP                                             │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│                                                             │
│       [A1: Causation] ─────┬──────> [Hypothesis]           │
│             │              │                               │
│             v              │                               │
│       [A2: Measurement] ───┘                               │
│             │                                               │
│             v                                               │
│       [A3: Population]                                     │
│                                                             │
│  If A1 fails: Entire branch invalidated                    │
│                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Key Features

#### 1. Automatic Assumption Extraction
During hypothesis intake, prompt for assumptions:
- "What are you assuming about causation?"
- "What measurement assumptions are you making?"
- "What population does this apply to?"

#### 2. Dependency Mapping
Users specify:
- "A1 is foundational — if wrong, everything fails"
- "A3 depends on A2"
- Visual graph of dependencies

#### 3. Cascade Analysis
If an assumption is invalidated:
- Show what else fails
- Update hypothesis status
- Suggest salvage options

#### 4. Assumption Testing Integration
Each assumption can:
- Generate tests in Test Queue
- Link to Evidence Ledger entries
- Update status based on evidence

#### 5. Assumption-Based Confidence
Confidence should factor in assumption status:
- All foundational assumptions validated → confidence boost
- Key assumption untested → confidence capped
- Foundational assumption invalidated → hypothesis fails

### Integration Points
- Hypothesis Intake → Captures assumptions
- Exclusion Test → Can target assumptions
- Evidence Ledger → Updates assumption status
- Research Brief → Shows assumption ladder

## Why This is Critical
Most hypotheses fail at their assumptions, not their core claim. By making assumptions explicit and testable:
- Users identify weak points early
- Cascade failures are understood before they happen
- Testing can be prioritized by assumption criticality
- The real burden of proof becomes clear

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Assumptions captured during intake
- [ ] Criticality classification works
- [ ] Dependency map visualized
- [ ] Cascade analysis shows failure paths
- [ ] Assumptions can be independently tested

 {"id":"brenner_bot-r2c5","title":"Task: Compute Dashboard Metrics","description":"# Task: Compute Dashboard Metrics

## Objective
Implement computation of all dashboard metrics by aggregating data from hypotheses, tests, and sessions.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/program-metrics.ts`

### Core Function
```typescript
export async function computeProgramMetrics(
  programId: string
): Promise<ProgramMetrics> {
  const program = await loadProgram(programId);
  if (!program) throw new Error(`Program ${programId} not found`);
  
  // Load all data for this program
  const hypotheses = await loadProgramHypotheses(program.sessions);
  const tests = await loadProgramTests(program.sessions);
  const scores = await Promise.all(program.sessions.map(scoreSession));
  
  // Compute hypothesis metrics
  const byState = groupBy(hypotheses, h => h.state);
  const activeCount = byState.active?.length ?? 0;
  const killedCount = byState.killed?.length ?? 0;
  const validatedCount = byState.validated?.length ?? 0;
  const denominator = killedCount + validatedCount + activeCount;
  
  // Compute test metrics
  const completedTests = tests.filter(t => t.status === 'completed');
  const blockedTests = tests.filter(t => t.status === 'blocked');
  
  // Compute session metrics
  const avgScore = mean(scores.map(s => s.totalScore));
  const trend = calculateTrend(scores);
  
  // Compute time metrics
  const firstSession = minBy(scores, s => s.scoredAt);
  const lastSession = maxBy(scores, s => s.scoredAt);
  const daysActive = daysBetween(firstSession.scoredAt, lastSession.scoredAt);
  
  return {
    programId,
    totalHypotheses: hypotheses.length,
    activeHypotheses: activeCount,
    killedHypotheses: killedCount,
    validatedHypotheses: validatedCount,
    dormantHypotheses: byState.dormant?.length ?? 0,
    killRate: denominator > 0 ? killedCount / denominator : 0,
    totalTests: tests.length,
    completedTests: completedTests.length,
    blockedTests: blockedTests.length,
    testExecutionRate: tests.length > 0 ? completedTests.length / tests.length : 0,
    sessionCount: program.sessions.length,
    averageSessionScore: avgScore,
    scoreTrend: trend,
    daysActive,
    lastActivityAt: lastSession.scoredAt,
  };
}
```

### Helper Functions
```typescript
// Load all hypotheses across program sessions
async function loadProgramHypotheses(sessionIds: string[]): Promise<Hypothesis[]>

// Load all tests across program sessions
async function loadProgramTests(sessionIds: string[]): Promise<TestRecord[]>

// Calculate trend from chronological scores
function calculateTrend(scores: SessionScore[]): 'improving' | 'declining' | 'stable'

// Group items by key
function groupBy<T>(items: T[], keyFn: (item: T) => string): Record<string, T[]>
```

### Derived Metrics
```typescript
export interface ProgramHealth {
  status: 'healthy' | 'stalled' | 'blocked' | 'unknown';
  reasons: string[];
}

export function assessProgramHealth(metrics: ProgramMetrics): ProgramHealth {
  const reasons: string[] = [];
  
  // Check for stalled programs
  if (metrics.daysActive > 7 && metrics.killRate < 0.1) {
    reasons.push('Low kill rate - consider designing more discriminative tests');
  }
  
  // Check for blocked progress
  if (metrics.blockedTests > metrics.completedTests) {
    reasons.push('Many blocked tests - address blockers before proceeding');
  }
  
  // Check for declining trend
  if (metrics.scoreTrend === 'declining') {
    reasons.push('Session scores declining - review method adherence');
  }
  
  if (reasons.length === 0) return { status: 'healthy', reasons: [] };
  if (reasons.some(r => r.includes('blocked'))) return { status: 'blocked', reasons };
  return { status: 'stalled', reasons };
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All metrics computed from underlying data
- [ ] Hypothesis funnel metrics accurate
- [ ] Test execution rate calculated
- [ ] Session score trend computed
- [ ] Days active calculated correctly
- [ ] Health assessment provides actionable status

## Dependencies
- Task: Define Research Program Schema
- Task: Implement Program Storage Layer
- Feature: Hypothesis Lifecycle States
- Feature: Test→Hypothesis Binding
- Feature: Brenner Method Scorecard

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew","title":"EPIC: Session Experience & UI - Orchestration, State Machines, Progress","description":"# Session Experience & UI - Orchestration, State Machines, Progress

## Purpose
Design and implement the user experience for running complete Brenner Loop sessions, from hypothesis intake through final research brief.

## The Session Flow

### Phase Progression
```
intake → sharpening → level_split → exclusion_test → 
object_transpose → scale_check → agent_dispatch → 
synthesis → evidence_gathering → revision → complete
```

Not all phases are required. User can:
- Skip operators that don't apply
- Jump directly to synthesis if operators completed
- Return to earlier phases to revise

### State Machine
```typescript
type SessionPhase =
  | 'intake'           // Initial hypothesis entry
  | 'sharpening'       // Socratic refinement
  | 'level_split'      // Σ operator
  | 'exclusion_test'   // ⊘ operator
  | 'object_transpose' // ⟳ operator
  | 'scale_check'      // ⊙ operator
  | 'agent_dispatch'   // Send to tribunal
  | 'synthesis'        // Aggregate responses
  | 'evidence_gathering' // Record tests & results
  | 'revision'         // Update hypothesis
  | 'complete';        // Generate final brief

interface SessionState {
  phase: SessionPhase;
  hypothesis: HypothesisCard;
  operatorResults: Partial<Record<OperatorType, OperatorResult>>;
  agentResponses: AgentResponse[];
  evidenceEntries: EvidenceEntry[];
  artifacts: GeneratedArtifact[];
  
  // Navigation
  canGoBack: boolean;
  canGoForward: boolean;
  availablePhases: SessionPhase[];
}
```

## UI Components

### Session Dashboard
Main view showing:
- Current phase (highlighted in timeline)
- Hypothesis card (always visible, editable in some phases)
- Phase-specific content area
- Progress indicators
- Quick actions

### Phase Timeline
Horizontal or vertical timeline showing:
- Completed phases (checkmark)
- Current phase (highlighted)
- Available phases (active)
- Skipped phases (dimmed)
- Locked phases (grayed out)

### Hypothesis Card Component
Always-visible card showing:
- Current statement
- Confidence score
- Version number
- Quick edit capability
- Evolution history popup

### Operator Panel
For each operator:
- Guided step-by-step UI
- Generated content display
- User selection/input areas
- Brenner quote sidebar

### Agent Tribunal Panel
Shows:
- Agent status (pending/running/complete)
- Response previews
- Expandable full responses
- Conflict/agreement indicators

### Evidence Ledger Panel
Displays:
- Timeline of tests
- Add new evidence button
- Confidence chart
- Robustness assessment

## Mobile Considerations
Session experience should work on tablet/mobile:
- Phase timeline becomes vertical
- Cards stack vertically
- Agent responses in accordion
- Full functionality preserved

## Keyboard Navigation
Power users should be able to:
- Navigate phases with arrow keys
- Trigger actions with shortcuts
- Jump to specific phases

## Persistence
Sessions auto-save:
- Every state change persisted
- Resume from where you left off
- No explicit save button needed

## Dependencies
- All other epics (orchestrates them all)

## Success Criteria
- Users can complete full sessions smoothly
- Progress is always clear
- Navigation is intuitive
- Mobile experience is usable
- Sessions survive browser refresh

│                                                                 │

```

## Key Components
1. **Phase Timeline**: Shows progress through session
2. **Phase Content Area**: Main interactive area
3. **Hypothesis Card**: Always-visible summary
4. **Brenner Sidebar**: Contextual quotes
5. **Navigation**: Previous/Next controls

## Responsive Behavior
- **Desktop**: Side-by-side layout as shown
- **Tablet**: Stacked layout, hypothesis card collapsible
- **Mobile**: Single column, cards in accordion

`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/SessionDashboard.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Layout clear and intuitive
- [ ] All key information visible
- [ ] Responsive across devices
- [ ] Phase switching is smooth

│                                                                 │

│  │ [Intake] → [Split] → [Exclude] → [Transpose] → [Scale] │   │
│  │    ●         ●         ◐            ○            ○      │   │
│  │ complete  complete  current      locked       locked    │   │
│  │                                                         │   │
│  │ → [Agents] → [Evidence] → [Synthesis] → [Brief]        │   │
│  │      ○           ○            ○           ○             │   │
│  │   locked      locked       locked      locked           │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────┐ ┌───────────────────────┐   │
│  │ CURRENT PHASE                 │ │ HYPOTHESIS CARD       │   │
│  │ ══════════════                │ │ ═══════════════       │   │
│  │                               │ │                       │   │
│  │ Exclusion Test (⊘)            │ │ H₂ (v3)      47%     │   │
│  │                               │ │                       │   │
│  │ "What would prove you wrong?" │ │ "Algorithm-driven..." │   │
│  │                               │ │                       │   │
│  │ [Phase-specific content]      │ │ [Edit] [History]      │   │
│  │                               │ │                       │   │
│  │                               │ └───────────────────────┘   │
│  │                               │                             │
│  │                               │ ┌───────────────────────┐   │
│  │                               │ │ 💬 BRENNER SAYS       │   │
│  │                               │ │                       │   │
│  │                               │ │ "Not merely unlikely  │   │
│  │                               │ │  — impossible..."     │   │
│  │                               │ │          — §89        │   │
│  └───────────────────────────────┘ └───────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  [← Previous]                                      [Next →]     │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Key Components
1. **Phase Timeline**: Shows progress through session
2. **Phase Content Area**: Main interactive area
3. **Hypothesis Card**: Always-visible summary
4. **Brenner Sidebar**: Contextual quotes
5. **Navigation**: Previous/Next controls

## Responsive Behavior
- **Desktop**: Side-by-side layout as shown
- **Tablet**: Stacked layout, hypothesis card collapsible
- **Mobile**: Single column, cards in accordion

## File Location
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/SessionDashboard.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Layout clear and intuitive
- [ ] All key information visible
- [ ] Responsive across devices
- [ ] Phase switching is smooth

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.2","title":"Build Phase Timeline Component","description":"# Build Phase Timeline Component

## Purpose
Create the visual timeline showing session phases and progress.

## Phase Flow
```
intake → sharpening → level_split → exclusion_test → 
object_transpose → scale_check → agent_dispatch → 
synthesis → evidence_gathering → revision → complete
```

## Visual States
- **Complete**: Filled circle (●), green
- **Current**: Half-filled (◐), blue, highlighted
- **Available**: Empty circle (○), gray, clickable
- **Locked**: Dimmed circle, no interaction
- **Skipped**: Strikethrough, dimmed

## Component API
```typescript
interface PhaseTimelineProps {
  phases: SessionPhase[];
  currentPhase: SessionPhase;
  completedPhases: SessionPhase[];
  availablePhases: SessionPhase[];
  skippedPhases: SessionPhase[];
  onPhaseClick: (phase: SessionPhase) => void;
}
```

## Responsive Behavior
- **Desktop**: Horizontal timeline with labels
- **Tablet**: Horizontal timeline, abbreviated labels
- **Mobile**: Vertical timeline or stepper

## Keyboard Navigation
- Arrow keys to navigate phases
- Enter to select phase
- Visual focus indicator

## Animation
- Smooth transition between phases
- Completion animation (checkmark appear)
- Progress indicator animation

## File Location
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/PhaseTimeline.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All phase states displayed correctly
- [ ] Phase switching works
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Responsive layout works
- [ ] Animations are smooth

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.3","title":"Implement Session State Machine","description":"# Implement Session State Machine

## Purpose
Create a formal state machine that governs session phase transitions.

## Why a State Machine
Session progression has complex rules:
- Some phases depend on others
- Some phases can be skipped
- Some phases can be revisited
- Invalid transitions should be impossible

A state machine makes this explicit and prevents bugs.

## State Definition
```typescript
const sessionMachine = createMachine({
  id: 'session',
  initial: 'intake',
  
  states: {
    intake: {
      on: { SUBMIT_HYPOTHESIS: 'sharpening' }
    },
    
    sharpening: {
      on: {
        REFINE: 'sharpening',  // Stay and refine more
        CONTINUE: 'level_split',
        SKIP_OPERATORS: 'agent_dispatch'
      }
    },
    
    level_split: {
      on: {
        COMPLETE: 'exclusion_test',
        SKIP: 'exclusion_test',
        BACK: 'sharpening'
      }
    },
    
    exclusion_test: {
      on: {
        COMPLETE: 'object_transpose',
        SKIP: 'object_transpose',
        BACK: 'level_split'
      }
    },
    
    object_transpose: {
      on: {
        COMPLETE: 'scale_check',
        SKIP: 'scale_check',
        BACK: 'exclusion_test'
      }
    },
    
    scale_check: {
      on: {
        COMPLETE: 'agent_dispatch',
        SKIP: 'agent_dispatch',
        BACK: 'object_transpose'
      }
    },
    
    agent_dispatch: {
      on: {
        RESPONSES_RECEIVED: 'synthesis',
        SKIP_AGENTS: 'evidence_gathering',
        BACK: 'scale_check'
      }
    },
    
    synthesis: {
      on: {
        CONTINUE: 'evidence_gathering',
        BACK: 'agent_dispatch'
      }
    },
    
    evidence_gathering: {
      on: {
        ADD_EVIDENCE: 'evidence_gathering',
        REVISE_HYPOTHESIS: 'revision',
        COMPLETE: 'complete',
        BACK: 'synthesis'
      }
    },
    
    revision: {
      on: {
        SAVE: 'evidence_gathering',
        RESTART_OPERATORS: 'level_split'
      }
    },
    
    complete: {
      type: 'final'
    }
  }
});
```

## Integration
Use XState or similar, or custom implementation:
```typescript
function useSessionMachine(initialState: SessionPhase) {
  // Returns current state, available transitions, send function
}
```

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/session-machine.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All states and transitions defined
- [ ] Invalid transitions impossible
- [ ] State persists with session
- [ ] Hook provides clean API

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.4","title":"Create Session List & Management Page","description":"# Create Session List & Management Page

## Purpose
Build the page where users see all their sessions and can create new ones.

## Page Layout
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  BrennerBot Lab                           [+ New Session]       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  Your Research Sessions                                         │
│  ═══════════════════════                                        │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ SM-2024-003                              2 hours ago    │   │
│  │ "Algorithm-driven content causes teen depression"       │   │
│  │ Phase: Evidence Gathering │ Confidence: 37% │ v3        │   │
│  │ [Continue] [Export] [Delete]                            │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ SM-2024-002                              1 day ago      │   │
│  │ "Meditation reduces anxiety through attention training"  │   │
│  │ Phase: Complete │ Confidence: 72% │ v2                  │   │
│  │ [View Brief] [Export] [Delete]                          │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ SM-2024-001                              3 days ago     │   │
│  │ "Remote work improves productivity"                     │   │
│  │ Phase: Exclusion Test │ Confidence: 65% │ v1            │   │
│  │ [Continue] [Export] [Delete]                            │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  [Load More]                                                    │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Features

### Session Card
Shows:
- Session ID
- Hypothesis preview
- Current phase
- Confidence score
- Last updated
- Quick actions

### Filtering & Sorting
- Sort by: Date, Confidence, Phase
- Filter by: Status (active, complete), Confidence range

### Actions
- **Continue**: Resume in-progress session
- **View Brief**: For complete sessions
- **Export**: Download as Markdown/PDF/JSON
- **Delete**: With confirmation

### New Session Flow
Click "+ New Session" → Hypothesis Intake form

## Route
`/lab` or `/sessions`

## File Location
`apps/web/src/app/lab/page.tsx`
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/SessionCard.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions listed with all info
- [ ] Sorting and filtering work
- [ ] Actions work correctly
- [ ] Delete has confirmation
- [ ] Pagination/infinite scroll works
